last_improvement.description: Reduced scrambler start from step 100 to step 50 for earlier disruption against 3 expanding opponents in 4-team format.
last_improvement.notes: Conservative timing adjustment (50-step shift). Maintains 50-step resource bootstrap buffer (vs 30-step minimum). Follows defensive tuning pattern from hotspot penalty. Cannot submit to freeplay - season not found or auth issue.

failed_attempts.description: Enhanced LLM prompt with prescriptive anti-stagnation role-change rules
failed_attempts.analysis: Too aggressive role switching disrupted team stability. Prescriptive rules (stalled miner→aligner, stalled aligner→miner) caused excessive role churn. May need softer approach or better stall detection threshold.

failed_attempts.description: Reduced early phase from 30 steps to 15 steps to get 5 aligners faster
failed_attempts.analysis: Ramping too early before economy stabilizes. Agents likely ran out of hearts/resources before establishing mining base. 30-step buffer seems necessary for resource bootstrapping.

failed_attempts.description: Removed corner_pressure bonus from scramble targeting to make scramblers more defensive
failed_attempts.analysis: Minimal impact. Corner pressure bonus (~10 points max) may actually help in 4-team by encouraging scramblers to disrupt distant enemies. Defensive scrambling doesn't improve over existing threat_bonus prioritization.

failed_attempts.description: Increased network bonus weight from 0.5 to 1.5 (3×) to prioritize chain-building near existing friendly junctions
failed_attempts.analysis: TOO AGGRESSIVE. Tripling network bonus caused agents to cluster around existing junctions instead of expanding. Leads to stagnant territory and missed opportunities. Expansion bonus (max 30) balanced against network bonus (max 2) for a reason - need both expansion and consolidation.

failed_attempts.description: Increased scrambler threat_bonus weight from 10.0 to 15.0 to better defend friendly junctions in 4-team format
failed_attempts.analysis: Increasing defensive priority hurt performance. Scramblers became too focused on protecting existing junctions rather than disrupting enemy expansion. Junction collapse pattern not addressed by over-defending. Current 10.0 weight appears optimal.

failed_attempts.description: Reduced _TARGET_CLAIM_STEPS from 30 to 20 for faster claim turnover in 4-team high-churn environment
failed_attempts.analysis: TOO SHORT. Reducing claim duration from 30 to 20 steps caused massive duplication. Agents re-claim same junctions multiple times before teammate arrives, wasting coordination. 30-step window (6 sec) is necessary for agents to actually reach claimed targets. Shorter claims = more churn, not better efficiency.

baseline_four_score.notes: With hotspot penalty (004) + early scrambler (007). Validated across 5 seeds. Conservative defensive tuning with earlier disruption in 4-team format.
